title: Guzmania cornuaulti
intro: Guzmania cornu-cervi, commonly referred to as the cornulata guzmania or Guzmania cornu-cervi, is a species of epiphytic flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Guzmania cornu-cervi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Guzmania cornu-cervi are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions in folk medicine.
taxonomy: Guzmania cornuaulti is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. It is a type of epiphyte, which means it grows on other plants for support. Guzmania cornuaulti is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is commonly found in cloud forests and is known for its bright green leaves and vibrant flowers. The classification of Guzmania cornuaulti also includes it being a member of the subfamily Tillandsioideae within the Bromeliaceae family.
